atoms.source_hunk_hint: @@ -0,0 +1,14 @@; async inspector crate manifest plus root workspace member/dependency wiring
atoms.source_snippet:
[package]
name = "ritual-async-inspector"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
description = "Shared inspector infrastructure for detecting async precompile calls"

[dependencies]
# Minimal dependencies - NO reth-evm, NO ritual-async
revm = { workspace = true }
alloy-primitives = { workspace = true }

# Logging
tracing = { workspace = true }

# root Cargo.toml wiring:
"crates/ritual-async-inspector/"
ritual-async-inspector = { path = "crates/ritual-async-inspector" }
atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence introduces `ritual-async-inspector` as a dependency-light shared crate for detecting async precompile calls during EVM execution. The manifest intentionally depends only on revm, alloy-primitives, and tracing through the workspace, and the root workspace wires it as a member and workspace dependency for validator and verifier consumers.

destinations.evidence: The source diff adds a 14-line new manifest at diff lines 42240-42259. The destination manifest preserves the same package metadata, dependency comments, three workspace dependencies, and absence of `ritual-*` dependencies.

The destination root Cargo.toml includes `crates/ritual-async-inspector/` in `workspace.members` and `ritual-async-inspector = { path = "crates/ritual-async-inspector" }` in `[workspace.dependencies]`.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The destination keeps the manifest text and root workspace wiring required by the source divergence: same crate identity, same dependency-light inspector dependency set, same workspace membership, and same workspace dependency path. That preserves the async inspector crate's build graph role exactly.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Removing the root workspace dependency or adding a `ritual-*` dependency to the inspector manifest would either break workspace path resolution for users of the crate or reintroduce the dependency cycle the source manifest explicitly avoids.
atoms.notes: This atom covers only the async inspector manifest and root workspace wiring, not any `crates/ritual-async-inspector/src/*` implementation files.

summary: The async inspector crate manifest and root workspace wiring are exact-port preserved: package metadata, minimal workspace dependency set, explicit cycle-avoidance dependency shape, workspace member entry, and workspace dependency path all match the source divergence.
notes: Queue entry verified as line 286: A	crates/ritual-async-inspector/Cargo.toml.
The source file diff starts at line 42240 and the Cargo.toml hunk ends at line 42259 before the next `crates/ritual-async-inspector/src/inspector.rs` header at line 42260.
Destination `crates/ritual-async-inspector/Cargo.toml` contains the same 14-line manifest body as the source-added file.
Destination root `Cargo.toml` includes `crates/ritual-async-inspector/` in workspace members and `ritual-async-inspector = { path = "crates/ritual-async-inspector" }` in workspace dependencies.
No review queue entry was required because the audited manifest and workspace wiring are preserved exactly.
